patch.value: Docstring gate: extend static-quality-gate to enforce public-API + data-contract coverage repo-wide

Extend scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs: (1) module-doc coverage over packages/ too (not just src/); (2) exported-declaration coverage includes exported const/let/var; (3) NEW exported-member coverage for members of exported interfaces, object-literal type aliases, and public members of exported classes; (4) NEW conservative trivial-stub check rejecting name-only/empty docstrings; all repo-wide across src/+packages/, thresholds default 100. Add full regression coverage to tests/unit/scripts/release/static-quality-gate.spec.ts (gate script is under the 100% coverage gate). This task is the enforcement oracle that defines DONE for the sibling fill tasks.

Gate exposes new pure checkers with 100% spec coverage; running the gate reports the new metrics at 100% once fill tasks land; boilerplate+trivial-stub checks green; no regression to existing gate metrics.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Extend static-quality-gate: packages module docs, exported consts, exported interface/type/class members, trivial-stub check

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Evidence — extended static-quality-gate with documentedSourceFiles (src+packages scope), exported-const coverage, checkExportedMemberDocstringCoverage, and checkTrivialDocstrings. Gate spec now 49 tests, verified 100% lines/branches/functions/statements on the gate script. Trivial-stub check calibrated to ZERO false positives across the ~2783 existing docstrings. Gate reports 3957 missing across 188 files (19 package module docs, 366 exported incl consts, 3572 members) for the sibling fill tasks.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: The trivial-docstring quality gate must subtract the symbol's own identifier tokens AND a tiny filler-word set from the prose, then flag only when zero meaningful words remain. Calibrated against all existing docstrings first (0 false positives) before enabling; enabling it repo-wide without that calibration would have failed the gate on legitimate terse docs.
